Descriptions.ShortDescription: Defines maximum altitude constraints to avoid imaging targets blocked by overhead obstructions.
Descriptions.LongDescription:
Maximum Horizon lets you build horizon profiles manually, import them from CSV, or extract from images so your sequences stop when a target exceeds your custom altitude limit.

## Use cases
- Apartment balcony or rooftop imaging where the sky is partially blocked overhead
- Backyards with trees, structures, or roof eaves limiting view in certain directions

## Features
- Multiple named horizon profiles (e.g. "Home", "Remote Site")
- Manual entry, CSV import, and image-based profile creation
- 'Loop until Maximum Horizon' condition that blocks the sequencer when a target rises above the configured maximum altitude
- Real-time visibility display (current altitude vs. maximum altitude, with profile name and ✓/✗ status)
- Global margin buffer for added safety
- Service interface (IMaximumHorizonService) for integration with planning plugins such as Target Scheduler

Profile storage: %localappdata%\NINA\Plugins\MaximumHorizon\Profiles\
